signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry
signals.text: Iran seizes and attacks two cargo ships trying to cross the Strait of Hormuz, driving safe-haven demand for JPY. USD/JPY at 159.23, testing the 160.00 level that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings; intervention risk rises on a sustained break above 160. The Fed-BoJ carry differential stands at 375bp, supporting USD. However, risk-off flows historically unwind JPY shorts — watch 157.00 as the first technical level on a sustained risk-off move.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail
signals.text: CFTC LF net long +41,675 for AUD, long%=72.0% — positioning near extreme bullish levels. Retail sentiment shows 89% short bias, an extreme contrarian signal. AUD/USD at 0.7159, holding above recent lows. Late-trend caution signal as market positioning and sentiment diverge — watch for LF delta turning negative or retail sentiment shifting to confirm.

signals.title: EUR/USD — CB + Technical
signals.text: ECB's Simkus states no rate hike expected in April, aligning with market bias. EUR/USD at 1.1747, testing the upper end of the recent range. The Fed-ECB rate differential stands at 175bp, structurally supporting USD. A sustained break above 1.1750 could signal further upside — watch 1.1800 as the next resistance level.

signals.title: GBP/USD — CB + Yield
signals.text: BoE at 3.75% vs Fed at 3.75% — 0bp GBP carry differential. GBP LF net long +28,426; AM constructive — positioning aligned with carry. GBP/USD at 1.3510, mid-range; 1.3600 is the next resistance on a continuation. Options neutral with ATM IV 9.5% near HV.

signals.title: NZD/USD — Correlation Break
signals.text: NZD/USD vs NZX 50 correlation break: corr=+0.661 vs 252d norm=-0.273 (z=+2.20σ). NZD/USD at 0.5912, supported by recent price action. This break suggests a potential regime shift — watch for confirmation from further data.
